info.name: Recruitment Auth API
info.description:
Complete test collection for the Recruitment Auth API.

## How to use
1. Import this collection AND the `recruitment-auth.postman_environment.json` file.
2. Select the **Recruitment Auth - Local** environment from the top-right dropdown.
3. Make sure the server is running (`npm run dev`).
4. Run requests in order, starting with **0. Health Check**.

## CSRF Note
The app uses double-submit CSRF protection. Every mutating request (POST/PATCH/DELETE) automatically fetches a fresh CSRF token via a **Pre-request Script** on the collection root before executing — you don't need to do anything manually.

exec: // Automatically fetch & inject a CSRF token before every state-changing
// (non-GET, non-OPTIONS) request so you never have to do it manually.
console.warn('⚠️  Could not extract CSRF token from response:', JSON.stringify(json));
// Collection-level test: log the response status for every request

request.description: Simple liveness probe. Should return `{ status: 'OK', timestamp: '...' }`.
Fetches a CSRF token. The collection pre-request script calls this automatically before every POST/PATCH, so you rarely need to run this manually.

exec: console.log('📧 Check email inbox — a verification link should be sent.');

request.description:
Register a new CANDIDATE account.

**Roles:** `CANDIDATE`, `RECRUITER`, `ADMIN`

**Password rules:** 8–128 chars, must contain uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special char.
Register a RECRUITER account.
Register an ADMIN account. Note: in a real system you'd protect this. For dev/testing this is open.
Deliberately sends a weak password to verify Zod validation returns 422 with field-level errors.

exec: // This will return 400/401 if the token variable is empty.
// Paste the real token from the verification email link into the {{emailVerifyToken}} env var.
pm.test('Response is not a server error', () => {

query.description: Paste the token from the verification email link here, or set the emailVerifyToken env variable.

request.description: Verifies a user's email. Copy the `token` query parameter from the verification link sent to your email (or Ethereal preview) and paste it into the `emailVerifyToken` environment variable.

exec: // 2FA required — save temp token for the 2FA verify step
console.log('🔐 2FA required. tempToken saved. Run "Verify 2FA" next.');

request.description:
Login as Candidate. On success:
- `accessToken` is saved to the environment (used for all protected routes)
- `refreshToken` is set in an **HttpOnly cookie** (invisible in Postman body, but Postman stores it automatically)

If 2FA is enabled, returns `{ requiresTwoFactor: true, tempToken: '...' }` instead — run **Verify 2FA** next.

exec: // Also set as the main access token for convenience
console.log('🎉 Admin login successful! adminAccessToken saved.');

request.description: Login as Admin. Saves the token to both `adminAccessToken` and `accessToken`.
Expects a 401 Unauthorized response. Also validates brute-force lockout behavior — repeated calls will eventually return 429.

Uses the HttpOnly `refreshToken` cookie (set automatically by Postman after login) to issue a new access token. Also rotates the refresh token (old one is revoked).

**Run Login first** to ensure the cookie is set.

exec: // Response should always look the same whether email exists or not

request.description: Sends a password reset email. Always returns a success-looking response (even for unknown emails) to prevent user enumeration attacks.

exec: pm.test('Response is identical to valid email response', () => {

request.description: Tests that a non-existent email still returns HTTP 200 with a safe message, confirming no user enumeration vulnerability.

exec: // Will fail with 400/401 if passwordResetToken env var is empty.
// Copy the token from the reset email link and set it in the env.

request.description:
Resets the password. Requires a valid reset token from the forgot-password email.

1. Run **Forgot Password**
2. Open the email (or Ethereal preview)
3. Copy the `token` query param from the reset link
4. Set it as `passwordResetToken` in the environment
5. Run this request

exec: // Confirm no sensitive fields are leaked

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile. Also saves `userId` to the environment for admin endpoint tests. Validates that sensitive fields (passwordHash, twoFactorSecret) are **not** returned.
Updates firstName and/or lastName and/or phone. All fields are optional — only provided fields are updated.
Hits a protected endpoint without a Bearer token. Expects 401 Unauthorized.

exec: console.log('👋 Logged out. accessToken cleared from environment.');

request.description:
Logs out the current user. This:
1. Revokes the refresh token in the DB
2. Clears the HttpOnly cookie
3. Clears `accessToken` from the Postman environment

exec: // Save the manual key for reference
console.log('📱 Scan the QR code OR manually enter key:', json.data.manualEntryKey);
console.log('QR code data URL is in json.data.qrCodeUrl');

request.description:
Generates a TOTP secret for the authenticated user and returns a QR code (data URL) + manual entry key.

**Next step:** Scan the QR code in Google Authenticator / Authy, then call **Enable 2FA** with the 6-digit code.

Activates 2FA for the account using the first valid TOTP code from the authenticator app.

1. Run **Setup 2FA** first
2. Open Google Authenticator / Authy
3. Enter the 6-digit code in the `totpCode` environment variable
4. Run this request

Completes a 2FA login. Use this when **Login** returns `{ requiresTwoFactor: true, tempToken: '...' }`.

- `tempToken` is saved automatically by the Login request
- `totpCode` must be the current 6-digit code from your authenticator app
Disables 2FA. Requires the current account password AND a valid TOTP code (double confirmation to prevent account takeover).

exec: // Auto-save first user's ID for other admin tests

query.description: Filter by role: CANDIDATE | RECRUITER | ADMIN
Filter by status: ACTIVE | SUSPENDED | PENDING_VERIFICATION

request.description:
Returns paginated list of all users. Supports query params:
- `page` (default: 1)
- `limit` (default: 20, max: 100)
- `search` - searches name and email
- `role` - filter by CANDIDATE | RECRUITER | ADMIN
- `status` - filter by ACTIVE | SUSPENDED | PENDING_VERIFICATION

Also auto-saves a non-admin `targetUserId` for the status-change test.
Tests the search and filter capabilities of the user list endpoint.

Returns full details of a specific user including login attempt and refresh token counts.

`targetUserId` is auto-populated by the **List All Users** test script.

item.name: Get User by ID — Not Found

request.description: Tests the 404 response for a non-existent user ID.

Suspends the target user. This also revokes all their active refresh tokens (forced logout).

**Status values:** `ACTIVE` | `SUSPENDED` | `PENDING_VERIFICATION`
Re-activates a previously suspended user.

exec: pm.test('Status is 400 (cannot change own status)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));

request.description:
Tries to suspend the admin's OWN account. Should return 400 due to the self-lockout guard.

`userId` is saved by the **Get My Profile** test after admin login.

query.description: Filter by event type e.g. USER_LOGIN, USER_REGISTERED, PASSWORD_RESET

request.description:
Returns paginated audit logs. Supports filtering by:
- `event` (e.g., `USER_LOGIN`, `USER_REGISTERED`, `PASSWORD_RESET`, `TOKEN_REFRESHED`)
- `userId`
- `startDate` / `endDate` (ISO 8601)

query.description: Filter attempts for a specific user

request.description: Returns paginated login attempt history. Useful for auditing brute-force attempts. Can filter by `userId`.
Uses the candidate `accessToken` (not admin) to hit an admin-only endpoint. Expects 403 Forbidden.
